commit.message:
fix: stage MSI files in bin/ to match wix.json glob paths

go-msi resolves file globs in wix.json relative to the workspace root.
wix.json declares payload paths as bin/MaximumTrainer.exe and bin/*.dll,
but the 'Prepare application folder for MSI' step was staging files into
application\bin\ instead. The glob bin/*.dll found zero files, causing
go-msi to insert the unexpanded literal '*.dll' into the WiX File/@Source
attribute, which triggered candle error CNDL0027. With product.wixobj
never produced, light.exe then failed with LGHT0103.

Fix: stage the exe and DLLs into bin\ at the workspace root so the
bin/*.dll glob in wix.json resolves correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Change to INSERT OR IGNORE — silently skips duplicates. Also deleted
the stale .index.db that had partial data from prior failed runs.

fix: robust ALB listener TG resolution for CodeDeploy blue/green deploys (#104)

* Initial plan

* fix: robust multi-strategy ALB listener TG sync for CodeDeploy blue/green

The sync step that aligns the ALB listener with the ECS primary task set
was silently skipping when the PRIMARY task set TG query returned empty,
causing CodeDeploy to fail with "Primary taskset target group must be
behind listener".

Added 4 fallback strategies:
1. PRIMARY task set TG (existing approach)
2. First task set TG regardless of status
3. CodeDeploy deployment group TG with healthy targets
4. First CodeDeploy deployment group TG (absolute fallback)

If all strategies fail, the step now fails hard instead of silently
proceeding to a doomed CodeDeploy deployment.

Applied consistently across deploy-prod, deploy-test, setup-prod, and
setup-test workflows.

- Refactor Stage 3 to run procurement review and FICA document waiting in parallel using Promise.all to prevent race conditions where FICA upload events were missed while waiting for procurement review.
- Refactor Stage 6 to wait for Risk Manager and Account Manager approvals in parallel, ensuring neither approval event is missed regardless of the order they are received.
- Add error logging for procurement check failures to ensure visibility without crashing the workflow immediately during testing.

Updates related workflows, validation schemas, and documentation to reflect this change, ensuring that the workflow no longer waits for the accountant letter before proceeding. Adjusts the validation agent to accommodate the new upload process.

Introduces a new skill for managing environment variables in Vercel's preview environment using the Vercel CLI. The documentation includes commands for adding, updating, listing, and removing variables, as well as pulling them locally and running commands with preview environment variables. * fix: resolve Stage 3 FICA gate deadlock caused by Inngest replay model

In Inngest, step.run() callbacks are NOT re-executed when a step result
is memoized (already completed). Setting context.documentsComplete = true
inside the 'mandate-verified' step callback meant this value was always
lost on replay, so Stage 3's ficaPromise always created a waitForEvent
for a second upload/fica.received event that never arrived.

Fix: return { documentsComplete: true } from the 'mandate-verified' step
and use mandateVerified.documentsComplete in Stage 3's ficaPromise gate.
Also replaced context.mandateType/mandateVolume (dead side effects inside
step callbacks) with mandateInfo.mandateType/mandateVolume from the
already-correct determine-mandate step return value.

Verified end-to-end: procurement CLEARED → Stage 3 FICA gate resolves
immediately via Promise.resolve (ficaSource: "stage2_documents_already_complete")
→ ITC/Sanctions/Reporter run → Stage 4 → human APPROVED → Stage 5.

commit.message:
feat: add 9 hookflow governance workflows for structure and quality protection

Add hookflows organized across 4 governance areas:

Import Structure (post-edit, blocking):
- enforce-esm-extensions: relative imports must use .js extensions
- enforce-dependency-direction: enforces layered architecture boundaries

Module & File Purity (pre-create/post-edit, blocking):
- enforce-types-purity: src/types/ must contain only type definitions
- enforce-file-naming: new files must use kebab-case naming
- enforce-test-placement: test files must live under tests/

Config & Output Protection (pre-edit, blocking):
- protect-config-files: blocks edits to tsconfig, tsup, vitest, package.json
- protect-dist-output: blocks direct changes to dist/

Code Quality (pre-edit + pre-commit, blocking):
- block-placeholder-code: blocks TODO, FIXME, stubs, skipped tests
- enforce-typecheck-on-commit: runs tsc --noEmit before commits

commit.message:
fix: tray version null — copy data before dispatch_async

The Go Init() calls C.CString then defer C.free, but initTray uses
dispatch_async which runs the block later on the main thread. By the
time the block executes, the C string is already freed (use-after-free).

Fix: copy version string and icon data to ObjC objects before
dispatch_async, so the block captures retained objects instead of
raw pointers to freed memory.

Fix bone rendering: sync outputs head position, add explicit joint anchors

Two fixes for visual bone stretching:

1. Sync was writing body midpoint position as bone_world translation,
   but skinning IBMs expect bone HEAD position. Now stores a per-bone
   body-local head_offset during creation and applies it in sync to
   recover the correct head position.

2. Added explicit anchor_a/anchor_b fields to bone_joint_desc_t so
   joint anchor positions can be exported from Blender rather than
   assumed from bone hierarchy. The exporter writes child bone HEAD
   as the anchor point for both parent and child bodies.

Files changed:
- bone_joint_desc.h: Added anchor_a[3], anchor_b[3], has_anchors
- phys_anim_entity.h: Added head_offsets array to entity struct
- phys_anim_entity_create.c: Store head offsets, use exported anchors
- phys_anim_entity_sync.c: Offset body midpoint → bone head in sync
- fskel_load.c: Parse anchor_a/anchor_b from joint_desc
- fskel_write.c: Write anchor data when has_anchors is set
- export_fskel.py: Export joint anchor positions

Fix md_parse_sequence_points document and rolling value bugs (#68)

Two bugs in sequence point parsing:

1. When the MethodDebugInformation row has a non-null Document
   column (the common case for single-document methods), the parser
   read the cursor into a local variable but never copied it to
   sequence_points->document. The field was left at an invalid row-0
   default, causing callers to fail when resolving the document name.

2. The rolling_il_offset, rolling_start_line, and rolling_start_column
   fields were not accumulated. The Portable PDB spec delta-encodes
   these values (absolute for the first record, signed deltas for
   subsequent records), but the parser stored raw deltas instead of
   running sums. This caused incorrect source locations for any
   sequence point after the first.

Fix both by:
  - Copying the Document column cursor directly when non-null.
  - Maintaining running accumulators for IL offset, start line,
    and start column across records.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
